data.title: Trump tells insiders Elon Musk will step back soon from lead government role: Politico
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has told people in his inner circle that Elon Musk will step back from his role as a governing partner in the coming weeks, Politico reported Wednesday. 

Trump and Musk in recent days had decided Musk will soon to return to his businesses and take on a supporting role in government, the report said, citing three unnamed Trump insiders. Trump remains pleased with Musk and his Department of Government Efficiency initiative, the report said. 

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares rose 2.8% to $275.75 in late-morning trade. They were lower earlier after Tesla’s Q1 deliveries plunged, with the EV maker posting 336,681 deliveries, below the consensus estimate of 377,000 vehicles. 

Broadly, stocks moved higher (SP500)(COMP:IND)(DJI) after muted moves ahead of the Trump administration’s announcement about potential reciprocal tariffs on trading partners worldwide.

data.title: T, TMUS top chart as communication services gainers in Q1; GOOGL, DIS among losers
data.description:
Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLC), which tracks S&P 500 Communication services sector, was one of the worst performers during the first quarter, showing a decline of nearly 1%.

The communication services sector, which consists of stocks including Alphabet (GOOG), Meta Platforms (META), Disney (DIS) and AT&T (T), is one of the sectors other than Information technology (XLK), consumer discretionary (XLY) and Industrials (XLI) that recorded a fall during the quarter. This is in comparison to the over 4% fall in the broader S&P500 Index.

Investors saw a volatile first quarter, with markets mostly remaining in the red due to uncertainties over tariff policies, higher inflation, geopolitical tensions and corporate earnings from companies.

Alphabet (GOOG) fell over 6% in reaction to the tech giant’s fourth quarter results. Even though the company narrowly topped profit expectations, it posted a few lackluster unit performances outside core beats in search and YouTube sales. On the other hand, Netflix’s (NFLX) shares hit record high, after investors were left impressed by the streaming giant’s much higher user growth than expected.

Industries Q1 Performance

Media & Entertainment fell about 10% in the first quarter, while Telecommunication Services gained 19%.

U.S. stock fund flow in and out of the sector was mostly average throughout the year, with noticeable dips during the month of March. The Communication Services-focused ETF had a net outflow of $399 million.

What are the top 5 movers in Q1?

  • Gainers: AT&T (T) +25.7%.
  • T-Mobile US (TMUS) +20.8%.
  • Fox (FOX) +15.3%.
  • Take-Two (TTWO) +13.9%.
  • Verizon (VZ) +13.8%.
  • Losers: Alphabet (GOOGL) -18.14%.
  • Walt Disney (DIS) -11.8%.
  • Omnicom (OMC) -7.9%.
  • Interpublic Group of Companies (IPG) -6.9%
  • Match Group (MTCH) -4.9%.

What Quantitative Measures say?

XLC received a Buy rating from Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, with a score of 4.33 out of 5. The sector got A+ for liquidity, expenses and momentum. However, it got a B+ for dividend and risk prospect.

What analysts expect?

One analyst surveyed by Seeking Alpha in the last 90 days gave the XLC a Buy rating.

According to FactSet analyst John Butters, Communication Services is one of the sectors which is expected to see the largest price increases in the next 12 months, with earnings and revenue for XLC expected to grow nearly 15% and 8% for CY25, respectively.

A recent Seeking Alpha analysis by Komal Sarwar noted “XLC offers a balanced portfolio of growth and defensive stocks, with lower downside risk and higher risk-adjusted returns” compared to its peers. However, Sarwar added that macroeconomic trends and consumers spending power are also a key to the sector's performance.

data.title: Qualcomm ticks up as chipmaker officially unveils Snapdragon 8s Gen 4
data.description:
• Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) shares ticked up fractionally on Wednesday as the San Diego-based chipmaker officially unveiled its Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 chipset.
  • The chipset, which is believed to use Taiwan Semiconductor's (TSM) 4 nanometer process, will offer support for Wi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 6.0, UFS 4.0 and other technologies. It
  • also offers a 49% improvement on graphics compared to the previous generation Snapdragon 8s Gen 3, Qualcomm added.
  • Qualcomm also said the new Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 has artificial intelligence-related improvements compared to the previous generation, with the system-on-a-chip showing a 44% performance improvement due to a better neural processing unit. 
  • The Cristiano Amon-led company did not say when the Snapdragon 8s Gen 4 chipset would be available, but it is expected to be used by several Android handset makers in the near future.

data.title: Grab Holdings makes a strategic move into Singapore's taxi market
data.description:
Ride-hailing giant Grab (NASDAQ:GRAB) landed a street-hail operator license in Singapore to set it up to become the sixth taxi operator in the nation. 

Grab (NASDAQ:GRAB) plans to introduce a taxi fleet in Singapore through its GrabRentals business to complement the private-hire cars available on its platform. "This enables us to address unmet consumer demand and improve ride availability, particularly during peak hours, late nights and in areas accessible only by taxis," a Grab (GRAB) spokesperson outlined.

The GrabCab business is expected to start on April 9. Under the license, GrabCab will have a grace period of three years to progressively expand its fleet to meet the minimum fleet size requirement of 800 taxis.

Morgan Stanley highlighted that Singapore is an important market for Grab (GRAB) as it accounted for 21% of 2024 revenue. The firm views the GrabCab development as a positive because it enables expansion of Grab's (GRAB) fleet in a supply-constrained market and complements its market leadership in ride-hailing. The business is also seen increasing Grab's (GRAB) addressable market within Singapore's point-to-point transport market by improving ride availability.

"We expect Grab to be able to ramp up its taxi fleet to the minimum requirement of 800 over the next 1–2 years," wrote MS analyst Divya Gangahar Kothiyal. The firm has an Overweight rating on Grab Holdings (GRAB) and a price target of $5.70.

Shares of Grab (GRAB) were down 0.7% in morning trading to $4.52.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: NCNO, TTEC, EWTX, BB, ANGO and more
data.description:
Stock futures dipped on Wednesday as investors braced for President Trump's impending tariff announcement amidst lingering market unease.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

TTEC Holdings (NASDAQ:TTEC) +34.9% – Shares climbed after the company said it's ready to engage with CEO Kenneth Tuchman on his $6.85 a share proposal. The special committee of the board reiterated its openness to engaging with Tuchman on a definitive transaction proposal, as stated Tuesday. This follows Tuchman's late September offer to buy the outstanding shares for $6.85 each, at a time when he owned about 58% of the company.

AngioDynamics (NASDAQ:ANGO) +10.1% – Shares jump after it reported fiscal second-quarter 2025 earnings, surpassing expectations with Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.08 beats by $0.05. Revenue of $72M (+9.1% Y/Y) beats by $1.7M and has updated full-year guidance. The MedTech segment saw a 22.2% increase in revenue. As the company previously stated, in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, the company expects to generate positive operating cash flow, ending with cash and cash equivalents around $55 million with zero debt. In addition, the company remains on track to achieve positive operating cash flow for the full year of fiscal 2026. The company now expects net sales to be in the range of $285 to $288 million, up from previously issued guidance of $282 to $288 million, representing growth between 5.3% to 6.4% over fiscal 2024 pro forma revenue of $270.7 million, and now expects adjusted loss per share in the range of $0.31 to $0.34, an improvement from prior guidance of a loss of $0.34 to $0.38. 

Cytokinetics (NASDAQ:CYTK) +13.3% – Shares up after its rivalEdgewise Therapeutics reported results for its treatment of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM), which led to a 28% decline in Edgewise’s stock. The readout comes at a time when Cytokinetics’ (NASDAQ:CYTK) lead asset, aficamten, targeted at the obstructive HCM, is currently undergoing FDA review with a decision expected on or before Sept. 26, 2025.

Cognyte Software (NASDAQ:CGNT) +20.7% – Shares up after the company reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter financial results. Non-GAAP EPS of $0.03 beat estimates by $0.02. Revenue of $94.51M, a 12.9% year-over-year increase, beat estimates by $1.36M. Adjusted EBITDA surged by 114% to $9.3 million, and short-term RPO increased to $335.3 million, indicating strong future revenue visibility. Total Backlog at the end of Q4 FYE25 was $415.5 million and short-term Backlog was $227.9 million. For FY26, the company expects revenue of $392 million at the midpoint with a range of +/-2%, representing approximately 12% growth from previous year revenue vs. $373.45M consensus. Adjusted EBITDA: Approximately $43 million at the midpoint of our revenue outlook. Non-GAAP Diluted EPS: $0.16 at the midpoint of our revenue outlook vs. $0.14 consensus.

Biggest stock losers

nCino (NASDAQ:NCNO) -31.7%  – Shares fell after the banking technology platform issued weaker-than-expected Q1 and FY2026 guidance following disappointing Q4 2025 earnings. nCino (NASDAQ:NCNO) released first-quarter guidance with non-GAAP EPS of $0.15-$0.16 and revenue of $138.75M-$140.75M, both falling short of analyst expectations. The company also provided fiscal 2026 guidance, with non-GAAP EPS of $0.66-$0.69 and revenue of $574.5M-$578.5M, again below consensus. In a move indicating its confidence, the board has approved a $100M stock buyback program. It reported Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 misses consensus by $0.07 and Revenue of $141.37M (+14.3% Y/Y) that beats consensus by $0.56M.

Edgewise Therapeutics (NASDAQ:EWTX) -23.6%  – Shares fell after a $200M capital raise announced by the company and reported results for its treatment of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM). The firm priced its underwritten offering of 9.9M shares at $20.13 apiece. The closing of the offering is expected to occur on April 3. Separately, Edgewise Therapeutics (NASDAQ:EWTX) posted topline data from a mid-stage trial for EDG-7500, an experimental therapy for a genetic heart disease known as hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM).

BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) -11.5%  – Shares fell after it reported a net loss of US$7.4 million in its fourth quarter, compared with a loss of US$56.2 million a year earlier, and issued weaker-than-anticipated guidance. BlackBerry beat fourth-quarter estimates with $0.03 adjusted EPS and $141.7M revenue, driven by QNX, Secure Communications, and Licensing. However, guidance fell short. Revenue was down 18.1% year-over-year, and operating cash flow was $42M. Despite the strong quarterly results, BlackBerry provided a weaker-than-expected outlook. First-quarter revenue is projected between $107 million and $115 million, below the consensus estimate of $127.99 million. Full-year fiscal 2026 revenue is expected to range from $504 million to $534 million, below the estimate of $548.35 million.

Newsmax (NYSE:NMAX) -45.8%  – Newsmax shares experienced a dramatic sequence: First, they surged 735% in their debut, triggering trading halts. This was followed by a 180% climb, closing Tuesday at $233. However, ahead of their third trading day, the stock plummeted 23% to $179 in premarket trading, reflecting the market's extreme volatility. The stock is currently down -45.8% overall.

More on related stocks:

  • nCino stock tumbles after Q4 earnings miss, soft Q1, full-year guidance

  • TTEC soars after saying it's willing to engage with CEO on $6.85 a share offer

  • Cognyte Software beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook

  • BlackBerry slides amid weak outlook for 2026

  • Edgewise Therapeutics falls after pricing $200M offering

data.title: Ecopetrol weighs buying Carlyle’s Colombia asset to boost reserves, Roa says
data.description:
Ecopetrol (NYSE:EC) is considering buying SierraCol Energy, Colombia's biggest independent oil producer, from Carlyle Group (CG), as it looks to boost reserves and production, company president Ricardo Roa told Bloomberg in an interview Wednesday.

The Ecopetrol (NYSE:EC) executive said the company is analyzing the option, but has not yet decided if it will present an offer, and declined to provide a potential amount.

Carlyle Group (CG) is seeking to sell SierraCol Energy for $1.5B, Reuters reported last month; the assets were purchased from Occidental Petroleum in 2020 for ~$825M.

Separately, Roa said Ecopetrol (NYSE:EC) plans to spend $500M-$700M to build a plant to produce sustainable aviation fuel, Reuters reported.

The new plant to be built at the Barrancabermeja refinery will start its development phase between this and next year, with production expected to begin by 2030, Roa said.

Ecopetrol (EC), which has set a $5.9B-$6.8B capital spending plan for this year, estimates it will produce 740K-750K boe/day this year and is focused on increasing reserves and production as Colombia's oil and natural gas reserves decline.

data.title: Stocks wobble as Trump's tariffs take center stage
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were muted Wednesday, as investors braced for U.S. President Donald Trump's plan to impose reciprocal tariffs on imports coming into the U.S. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) +0.1% and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was flat after each fell more than 1% early in the session. The Dow (DJI) +0.2% after losing nearly 1%. 

In the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 1 basis points at 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) turned higher, up 2 basis points to 3.89%.

Investors were waiting for details on the Trump administration's “Liberation Day” plan to impose large-scale tariffs on trading partners. The administration so far has said tariffs will "start with all countries" and will be "effective immediately."

A range of rates may be revealed, dependent on trade barriers or taxation levels. President Trump was slated to appear in the Rose Garden at 4 pm ET. The U.S. Trade Representative's office was preparing a third option for tariffs that would be lower than a potential 20% universal levy, The Wall Street Journal reported Tuesday.

Ahead of the tariff announcement, the ADP National Employment Report released showed March U.S. private sector employment came in at +155K vs. +120K consensus and +84K prior (revised from +77K). 

Elsewhere in markets, the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) was down 0.2% at 104.01. "The harsher the [tariffs] announcement is perceived to be, the greater the likelihood that the dollar will weaken, as traders factor in a scenario of slower US growth, potentially forcing the Federal Reserve to cut rates faster than previously expected," Ricardo Evangelista, senior analyst at ActivTrades, said. 

For the Federal Reserve, a strategy of “looking through” tariffs is weaker now than during Trump 1.0 because inflation is running above target, Dario Perkins, managing director of global macro at GlobalData TS Lombard, said. 

The Fed “won’t cut interest rates until there are unambiguous signs of weakness in the economy (particularly in the labor market). And that is bad news for financial markets, particularly compared to the situation in 2024 when the Fed was cutting rates pre-emptively,” Perkins said in a note.

“A Fed that is waiting for something to go wrong in the economy is by definition more reactive, and in danger of falling behind the curve," Perkins said. 

Besides new tariffs, 25% levies on cars not made in the U.S. and secondary tariffs on nations that purchase crude oil from Venezuela were set to go into effect this week, the Trump administration has said. 

Wall Street's major averages finished mixed Tuesday in the jumpstart of Q2 action.

data.title: Strategy, Core Scientific snag Buy ratings as Clear Street starts coverage
data.description:
Clear Street recently initiated coverage on Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) shares with a Buy rating as the Michael Saylor-founded bitcoin (BTC-USD) development company's "ability to grow its BTC per share, net of dilution, creates real value for shareholders, beyond asset class exposure."

The company is widely regarded as a levered, high-beta exposure to bitcoin (BTC-USD), offering debt and equity to fund its (usually weekly) BTC purchases.

But, there are risks to Strategy's (NASDAQ:MSTR) treasury management program, with the obvious one being a meaningful drop in bitcoin (BTC-USD) adoption and the token's price. "In addition, if MSTR’s access to capital markets contracts, its ability to generate BTC yield could be impaired," analyst Brian Dobson wrote in a note.

In a separate note, he also launched a Buy recommendation on bitcoin (BTC-USD) miner Core Scientific (NASDAQ:CORZ), owing to its pivot to the high-performance computing infrastructure business.

The transition to "AI compute resulted in $10bn of long-term contracted revenue, primarily with CoreWeave(CRWV), positioning the company for stable, high-margin growth," Dobson wrote.

But CORZ's dependency on CoreWeave (CRWV) for growth is exactly what's preventing SA analyst Mike Fay from jumping into the stock.

In Wednesday late morning trading, CORZ gained 2.7%, while MSTR inched up 0.2%.

data.title: Paramount Global cut to hold at Deutsche Bank on ad outlook
data.description:
Paramount Global (NASDAQ:PARA)  was cut to hold from buy at Deutsche Bank on risks to the advertising outlook and more attractive investment alternatives.

``We see the risk/reward as more balanced now, especially in light of what we see as increasing risk to the advertising outlook due to macroeconomic factors," Deutsche Bank analyst Brian Kraft, who also cut the Paramount (NASDAQ:PARA) price target to $12 from $15, wrote in a note. 

Kraft had believed that Paramount (PARA) shares were attractive, with Class B holders entitled to sell half of their shares for $15 in the Skydance deal, though the advertising outlook is now more uncertain due to macroeconomic factors. 

Companies that are in a better position to weather a cyclical downturn include Fox (FOXA) and Formula One (FWONA) or have more attractive valuations, with more clarity into their outlooks  include Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and Disney (DIS).

Shares of Paramount (PARA) ticked lower by 0.4% on Wednesday.

data.title: Rocket Cos. upgraded to Buy at Deutsche Bank, to Market Perform at KBW
data.description:
Rocket Companies (NYSE:RKT) stock jumped 15% as Deutsche Bank and KBW upgraded the lender's stock after it agreed to acquire Mr. Cooper (COOP) in $9.4B all-stock deal earlier this week.

Deutsche Bank analyst Mark DeVries upgraded RKT to Buy from Hold, expecting the acquisition will "provide a big step toward the ambitious 2027 market share targets laid out last September (at least the 20% refi share target)."

He lifted its price target to $16 from $14. He sees more upside potential in a lower rate environment, where the revenue synergies from boosting Mr. Cooper's recapture "should be more pronounced."

KBW analyst Bose George raised Rocket (NYSE:RKT) to Market Perform from Underperform, as he sees the Mr. Cooper (COOP) deal creating a more balanced business model, providing more confidence in earnings estimates.

"RKT's 2027 market share goals appear more attainable, considering the COOP deal would roughly quadruple RKT's servicing UPB to ~$2.15T and position RKT to utilize its industry-leading recapture capabilities in any declining rate environment," George wrote in a note to clients.

The recommendations of Hold or better from Deutsche Bank and KBW contrast with the SA Quant rating of Sell. The average SA Analyst rating  is Buy, agreeing with the Deutsche Bank recommendation, while the average Wall Street rating  of Hold aligns with KBW's assessment.
